---
title: "Laura Rose Werner"
---

## About the Author {#sec-laura}

Laura is a graduate student at Brooklyn College whose work reflects a strong emphasis on reproducible research, statistical reasoning, and professional data communication. Her portfolio is designed not just as a collection of assignments, but as a polished analytical artifact suitable for academic and professional audiences.

## Learning Journey

This portfolio presents a series of applied data analyses developed using Quarto. Across chapters, Laura works with diverse real-world datasets, including crime data, civic complaints, demographic indicators, sports analytics, and behavioral data.

Each project follows a consistent analytical workflow: - Data cleaning and preparation\
- Visualization and exploration\
- Statistical testing or modeling\
- Clear interpretation of results

Her work demonstrates the ability to move systematically from raw data to meaningful, well-communicated insights.

## Reflection & Growth

A defining feature of Laura’s work is her transition from assignment-based analysis to intentional, structured communication. This portfolio represents an evolution from an earlier Bookdown project into a refined Quarto-based publication.

Rather than simply completing analyses, she focused on improving: - clarity of interpretation\
- organization of code and outputs\
- reproducibility of results\
- overall presentation and readability

This shift reflects a deeper understanding of what it means to produce high-quality analytical work.

## Strengths & Achievements

Laura’s portfolio stands out for its breadth of applied analyses and its consistency in execution. Projects include:

- NYC crime and shooting analyses\
- NYC 311 complaint data with interactive Shiny components\
- Income prediction using regression models\
- Streaming behavior using chi-square analysis\
- Sports analytics using NBA data

Across all projects, she demonstrates the ability to produce publication-quality tables and visualizations while maintaining fully reproducible workflows.

## Key Takeaways

- Developed strong reproducible workflows using Quarto\
- Strengthened statistical reasoning and interpretation\
- Built experience across multiple domains and datasets\
- Improved clarity and professionalism in analytical communication\
- Created a portfolio suitable for academic and professional review

## What This Portfolio Demonstrates

Laura’s work highlights the ability to go beyond running statistical tests and instead focus on communicating meaningful, well-supported insights. Her portfolio emphasizes transparency, structure, and reproducibility—key components of modern data analysis and research.

> “This portfolio demonstrates my ability to move beyond running statistical tests and toward communicating meaningful insights in a structured, reproducible way.”

## View Full Portfolio

👉 [**Explore Laura’s Full Portfolio Book**](https://019c7ca5-9872-3f70-0ff8-780f5f216b2e.share.connect.posit.cloud/)
